Метод объединяет управляемую генерацию изображений с Direct Preference Optimization для выравнивания управления, curriculum-based добычей сложных примеров и многоэтапной фильтрацией качества. Созданный датасет из 500,000+ выборок демонстрирует на 76% улучшение метрик качества изображений по сравнению с рендерингом и достигает производительности, сравнимой или превосходящей модели, обученные на реальных и традиционных синтетических данных. Комбинация PoseDreamer с существующими синтетическими датасетами показывает лучший результат, чем объединение реальных и синтетических данных.

en.title: Revolutionizing 3D Mesh Datasets with PoseDreamer
en.desc: PoseDreamer is a novel approach that uses diffusion models to create large-scale synthetic datasets of 3D human meshes, addressing the challenges of acquiring labeled data. Traditional datasets often lack diversity or photorealism, making them less effective for training machine learning models. By employing techniques like Direct Preference Optimization and curriculum-based hard sample mining, PoseDreamer ensures high-quality image generation while maintaining accurate 3D annotations. The results show that models trained on PoseDreamer outperform those trained on both real-world and conventional synthetic datasets, highlighting its effectiveness in enhancing dataset utility.

zh.title: PoseDreamer：高质量合成3D人类网格数据的生成新方法
zh.desc: PoseDreamer 是一种新颖的生成模型，利用扩散模型生成大规模的合成3D人类网格数据集。与传统方法相比，它在图像质量和性能上有显著提升。该方法通过可控的图像生成、直接偏好优化、基于课程的困难样本挖掘和多阶段质量过滤等技术，确保3D标签与生成图像之间的对应关系。最终，PoseDreamer 生成了超过50万个高质量的合成样本，模型训练效果优于传统的真实和合成数据集。

papers.title: How Auditory Knowledge in LLM Backbones Shapes Audio Language Models: A Holistic Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Large language models (LLMs) have been widely used as knowledge backbones of Large Audio Language Models (LALMs), yet how much auditory knowledge they encode through text-only pre-training and how this affects downstream performance remains unclear. We study this gap by comparing different LLMs under two text-only and one audio-grounded setting: (1) direct probing on AKB-2000, a curated benchmark testing the breadth and depth of auditory knowledge; (2) cascade evaluation, where LLMs reason over text descriptions from an audio captioner; and (3) audio-grounded evaluation, where each LLM is fine-tuned into a Large Audio Language Model (LALM) with an audio encoder. Our findings reveal that auditory knowledge varies substantially across families, and text-only results are strongly correlated with audio performance. Our work provides empirical grounding for a comprehensive understanding of LLMs in audio research.

ru.title: Текстовые знания LLM как предсказатель акустического понимания
ru.desc: Исследование изучает, насколько хорошо большие языковые модели (LLM) понимают звуковую информацию, когда обучены только на текстах. Авторы предлагают три метода оценки: прямое тестирование на специальном бенчмарке AKB-2000, каскадный анализ через текстовые описания аудио и fine-tuning LLM с аудиокодировщиком для создания больших аудиолингвистических моделей. Результаты показывают, что различные семейства LLM обладают разным уровнем акустических знаний, и производительность на текстовых тестах хорошо коррелирует с результатами на звуковых задачах. Работа предоставляет эмпирическую основу для понимания роли LLM в обработке аудио.

en.title: Unlocking Auditory Knowledge in Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates how much auditory knowledge is encoded in Large Language Models (LLMs) that are pre-trained only on text. The authors compare various LLMs using different evaluation methods, including direct probing on a benchmark and cascade evaluation with audio descriptions. They find that the amount of auditory knowledge varies significantly among different LLM families, and that performance on text-only tasks is closely linked to how well these models perform on audio tasks. This research enhances our understanding of LLMs' capabilities in audio-related applications.

zh.title: 揭示大型语言模型的听觉知识
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在音频语言模型（LALMs）中的知识编码能力。我们通过对比不同的LLMs，在文本和音频基础的设置下进行评估，发现它们的听觉知识在不同模型之间差异显著。研究表明，文本训练结果与音频性能之间存在强相关性。我们的工作为理解LLMs在音频研究中的应用提供了实证基础。

papers.title: VectorGym: A Multitask Benchmark for SVG Code Generation, Sketching, and Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract VectorGym presents a comprehensive benchmark suite for scalable vector graphics encompassing text-to-svg generation, sketch-to-svg conversion, complex svg editing, and visual understanding tasks with human-annotated datasets and a multi-task reinforcement learning approach.  AI-generated summary We introduce VectorGym, a comprehensive benchmark suite for Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) that spans generation from text and sketches, complex editing, and visual understanding. VectorGym addresses the lack of realistic, challenging benchmarks aligned with professional design workflows. Our benchmark comprises four tasks with expert human-authored annotations: the novel Sketch2SVG task (VG-Sketch); a new SVG editing dataset (VG-Edit) featuring complex, multi-step edits with higher-order primitives; Text2SVG generation (VG-Text); and SVG captioning (VG-Cap). Unlike prior benchmarks that rely on synthetic edits, VectorGym provides gold-standard human annotations that require semantic understanding and design intent. We also propose a multi-task reinforcement learning approach that jointly optimizes across all four tasks using rendering-based rewards. Our method, built on GRPO with curriculum learning, trains a Qwen3-VL 8B model that achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models, surpassing much larger models including Qwen3-VL 235B and matching GPT-4o. We also introduce a VLM-as-a-Judge metric for SVG generation, validated through human correlation studies. Our evaluation of frontier VLMs reveals significant performance gaps, positioning VectorGym as a rigorous framework for advancing visual code generation. VectorGym is publicly available on huggingface.co/datasets/ServiceNow/VectorGym.

ru.title: Единый бенчмарк для обучения моделей генерировать и редактировать векторную графику
ru.desc: VectorGym представляет комплексный бенчмарк для масштабируемой векторной графики, включающий четыре задачи: генерацию SVG из текста и эскизов, сложное редактирование и визуальное понимание. Авторы собрали золотой стандарт аннотаций с помощью экспертов вместо синтетических данных, что позволило создать более реалистичные и сложные примеры, отражающие профессиональные дизайнерские рабочие процессы. Предложен подход многозадачного обучения с подкреплением, который одновременно оптимизирует все четыре задачи на основе rendering-based reward функций с использованием curriculum learning. Модель Qwen3-VL 8B, обученная таким образом, достигает лучших результатов среди открытых моделей и соответствует производительности GPT-4o, при этом значительно уступая по размеру закрытым системам.

en.title: VectorGym: Advancing SVG Generation with Human-Centric Benchmarks
en.desc: VectorGym is a benchmark suite designed for scalable vector graphics (SVG) that includes tasks like generating SVG from text, converting sketches to SVG, and editing SVG files. It addresses the need for realistic benchmarks that reflect professional design workflows by providing human-annotated datasets. The suite features four main tasks: Sketch2SVG, SVG editing, Text2SVG generation, and SVG captioning, all requiring a deep understanding of design intent. Additionally, it employs a multi-task reinforcement learning approach to optimize performance across these tasks, achieving state-of-the-art results compared to larger models.

zh.title: VectorGym：提升SVG生成的基准与性能
zh.desc: VectorGym是一个全面的基准套件，专注于可扩展矢量图形（SVG），涵盖了从文本生成SVG、草图转换为SVG、复杂的SVG编辑以及视觉理解任务。该基准解决了缺乏与专业设计工作流程相一致的真实且具有挑战性的基准的问题。它包含四个任务，均由专家人工注释，包括新颖的Sketch2SVG任务、复杂的SVG编辑数据集、文本到SVG生成和SVG标题生成。我们还提出了一种多任务强化学习方法，通过基于渲染的奖励共同优化这四个任务，训练出的模型在开源模型中表现出色。

papers.title: Distilling Human-Aligned Privacy Sensitivity Assessment from Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models are distilled into lightweight encoders for efficient privacy evaluation of textual data while maintaining strong human agreement and reducing computational costs.  AI-generated summary Accurate privacy evaluation of textual data remains a critical challenge in privacy-preserving natural language processing. Recent work has shown that large language models (LLMs) can serve as reliable privacy evaluators, achieving strong agreement with human judgments; however, their computational cost and impracticality for processing sensitive data at scale limit real-world deployment. We address this gap by distilling the privacy assessment capabilities of Mistral Large 3 (675B) into lightweight encoder models with as few as 150M parameters. Leveraging a large-scale dataset of privacy-annotated texts spanning 10 diverse domains, we train efficient classifiers that preserve strong agreement with human annotations while dramatically reducing computational requirements. We validate our approach on human-annotated test data and demonstrate its practical utility as an evaluation metric for de-identification systems.

ru.title: Компактные модели для приватной оценки текстов без больших затрат
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали метод дистилляции больших языковых моделей в легковесные энкодеры для оценки приватности текстовых данных. Компактные модели с 150 миллионами параметров сохраняют высокое соответствие человеческим оценкам, при этом значительно снижая вычислительные затраты. Подход обучен на большом наборе размеченных данных из 10 различных предметных областей и валидирован на аннотированных тестовых данных. Эффективные классификаторы могут использоваться для практической оценки качества систем деидентификации и анализа конфиденциальности в масштабе.

en.title: Efficient Privacy Evaluation with Lightweight Encoders
en.desc: This paper presents a method to create smaller, more efficient models from large language models (LLMs) for evaluating privacy in text data. The authors focus on distilling the capabilities of a large model, Mistral Large 3, into lightweight encoders with significantly fewer parameters. By training these encoders on a diverse dataset of privacy-annotated texts, they ensure that the models maintain a high level of agreement with human evaluations. The resulting classifiers are not only computationally efficient but also practical for real-world applications in privacy-preserving natural language processing.

zh.title: 轻量级编码器：高效隐私评估的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种将大型语言模型（LLM）提炼为轻量级编码器的方法，以高效评估文本数据的隐私性，同时保持与人类判断的一致性并降低计算成本。研究表明，LLM可以作为可靠的隐私评估工具，但其计算成本高，限制了在实际应用中的使用。我们通过将Mistral Large 3模型的隐私评估能力提炼到仅有150M参数的轻量级编码器，解决了这一问题。最终，我们在多个领域的隐私标注文本数据上训练了高效分类器，并验证了其在去标识化系统评估中的实用性。

papers.title: WorldFlow3D: Flowing Through 3D Distributions for Unbounded World Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract WorldFlow3D generates unbounded 3D worlds by modeling 3D data distributions as a flow matching problem, achieving rapid convergence and high-quality generation with controllable geometric and texture properties.  AI-generated summary Unbounded 3D world generation is emerging as a foundational task for scene modeling in computer vision, graphics, and robotics. In this work, we present WorldFlow3D, a novel method capable of generating unbounded 3D worlds. Building upon a foundational property of flow matching - namely, defining a path of transport between two data distributions - we model 3D generation more generally as a problem of flowing through 3D data distributions, not limited to conditional denoising. We find that our latent-free flow approach generates causal and accurate 3D structure, and can use this as an intermediate distribution to guide the generation of more complex structure and high-quality texture - all while converging more rapidly than existing methods. We enable controllability over generated scenes with vectorized scene layout conditions for geometric structure control and visual texture control through scene attributes. We confirm the effectiveness of WorldFlow3D on both real outdoor driving scenes and synthetic indoor scenes, validating cross-domain generalizability and high-quality generation on real data distributions. We confirm favorable scene generation fidelity over approaches in all tested settings for unbounded scene generation. For more, see https://light.princeton.edu/worldflow3d.

ru.title: Генерация безграничных 3D-миров через поток распределений
ru.desc: WorldFlow3D представляет новый метод для генерации неограниченных 3D-миров, основанный на подходе flow matching, который моделирует распределение 3D-данных как задачу транспортировки между двумя распределениями. Метод работает в скрытом пространстве без явного кодирования и генерирует высокачественную 3D-структуру с точной геометрией и текстурами, сходясь быстрее, чем существующие методы. Авторы добавили возможность контролировать генерацию сцен через векторизованные условия макета сцены для управления геометрией и визуальными атрибутами для контроля текстуры. Метод продемонстрировал эффективность на реальных данных уличных сцен и синтетических внутренних сцен, подтверждая обобщаемость и высокое качество генерации.

en.title: Flowing into Infinite 3D Worlds!
en.desc: WorldFlow3D is a new method for creating limitless 3D environments by treating the generation process as a flow matching problem between 3D data distributions. This approach allows for quick convergence and the production of high-quality 3D structures with customizable geometric and texture features. By using a latent-free flow technique, it generates accurate 3D shapes and can guide the creation of more intricate structures and textures. The method has been tested on both real and synthetic scenes, demonstrating its effectiveness and ability to generate realistic environments across different domains.

zh.title: 无限三维世界生成的新方法
zh.desc: WorldFlow3D是一种新颖的方法，能够生成无限的三维世界。它通过将三维数据分布建模为流匹配问题，实现了快速收敛和高质量的生成。该方法允许对生成场景的几何结构和视觉纹理进行控制，适用于真实和合成场景。实验结果表明，WorldFlow3D在各种设置下都能提供优越的场景生成效果。

papers.title: CREval: An Automated Interpretable Evaluation for Creative Image Manipulation under Complex Instructions
papers.abstract: Abstract A fully automated question-answer based evaluation pipeline and comprehensive benchmark are introduced for assessing creative image manipulation tasks under complex instructions, demonstrating strong alignment with human judgments.  AI-generated summary Instruction-based multimodal image manipulation has recently made rapid progress. However, existing evaluation methods lack a systematic and human-aligned framework for assessing model performance on complex and creative editing tasks. To address this gap, we propose CREval, a fully automated question-answer (QA)-based evaluation pipeline that overcomes the incompleteness and poor interpretability of opaque Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) scoring. Simultaneously, we introduce CREval-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark specifically designed for creative image manipulation under complex instructions. CREval-Bench covers three categories and nine creative dimensions, comprising over 800 editing samples and 13K evaluation queries. Leveraging this pipeline and benchmark, we systematically evaluate a diverse set of state-of-the-art open and closed-source models. The results reveal that while closed-source models generally outperform open-source ones on complex and creative tasks, all models still struggle to complete such edits effectively. In addition, user studies demonstrate strong consistency between CREval's automated metrics and human judgments. Therefore, CREval provides a reliable foundation for evaluating image editing models on complex and creative image manipulation tasks, and highlights key challenges and opportunities for future research.

ru.title: Автоматическая оценка творческого редактирования изображений через вопросно-ответный диалог
ru.desc: В работе предлагается CREval — полностью автоматизированный конвейер оценки на основе вопросов и ответов для оценки моделей творческого редактирования изображений по сложным инструкциям. Авторы разработали CREval-Bench — комплексный бенчмарк с более чем 800 образцами редактирования и 13000 оценочных запросов, охватывающий девять творческих измерений редактирования. Система преодолевает ограничения непрозрачных мультимодальных больших языковых моделей, обеспечивая лучшую интерпретируемость и полноту оценки. Проведённые пользовательские исследования подтвердили высокую согласованность автоматических метрик CREval с человеческими суждениями.

en.title: CREval: A New Standard for Evaluating Creative Image Manipulation
en.desc: This paper presents CREval, an automated evaluation pipeline that uses question-answering to assess the performance of models in creative image manipulation tasks. It addresses the limitations of current evaluation methods by providing a systematic framework that aligns with human judgments. The authors also introduce CREval-Bench, a benchmark with over 800 editing samples and 13,000 evaluation queries, covering various creative dimensions. The findings indicate that while closed-source models perform better overall, all models face challenges in executing complex edits, highlighting areas for future improvement in the field.

zh.title: CREval：评估创意图像处理的新标准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种完全自动化的基于问答的评估管道和综合基准，用于评估复杂指令下的创意图像处理任务，显示出与人类判断的强一致性。我们提出的CREval管道克服了现有多模态大语言模型评分的不足，提供了系统化和人性化的评估框架。同时，CREval-Bench是专为复杂指令下的创意图像处理设计的基准，涵盖了三个类别和九个创意维度，包含超过800个编辑样本和13000个评估查询。通过这一管道和基准，我们系统地评估了多种最先进的开源和闭源模型，结果显示尽管闭源模型在复杂创意任务上通常表现更好，但所有模型在有效完成这些编辑方面仍然面临挑战。

papers.title: ArtHOI: Taming Foundation Models for Monocular 4D Reconstruction of Hand-Articulated-Object Interactions
papers.abstract: Abstract ArtHOI presents an optimization-based framework that integrates foundation model priors to reconstruct 4D human-articulated-object interactions from single monocular RGB videos using adaptive sampling refinement and multimodal language model guidance.  AI-generated summary Existing hand-object interactions (HOI) methods are largely limited to rigid objects, while 4D reconstruction methods of articulated objects generally require pre-scanning the object or even multi-view videos. It remains an unexplored but significant challenge to reconstruct 4D human-articulated-object interactions from a single monocular RGB video. Fortunately, recent advancements in foundation models present a new opportunity to address this highly ill-posed problem. To this end, we introduce ArtHOI, an optimization-based framework that integrates and refines priors from multiple foundation models. Our key contribution is a suite of novel methodologies designed to resolve the inherent inaccuracies and physical unreality of these priors. In particular, we introduce an Adaptive Sampling Refinement (ASR) method to optimize object's metric scale and pose for grounding its normalized mesh in world space. Furthermore, we propose a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) guided hand-object alignment method, utilizing contact reasoning information as constraints of hand-object mesh composition optimization. To facilitate a comprehensive evaluation, we also contribute two new datasets, ArtHOI-RGBD and ArtHOI-Wild. Extensive experiments validate the robustness and effectiveness of our ArtHOI across diverse objects and interactions. Project: https://arthoi-reconstruction.github.io.

ru.title: Реконструкция 4D взаимодействия рук и шарнирных объектов из монокулярного видео
ru.desc: ArtHOI представляет собой оптимизационный фреймворк для реконструкции 4D взаимодействий человека с шарнирными объектами из однокадровых RGB-видео, используя прямы от фундаментальных моделей. Метод вводит адаптивное уточнение выборки для оптимизации масштаба и позы объекта в мировом пространстве. Предложен новый подход с использованием мультимодальной языковой модели для выравнивания рук и объектов, где информация о контактах служит ограничениями при оптимизации. Авторы создали два новых датасета и продемонстрировали эффективность метода на разнообразных объектах и взаимодействиях.

en.title: Revolutionizing 4D Human-Articulated-Object Interaction Reconstruction
en.desc: ArtHOI is a novel framework that reconstructs 4D interactions between humans and articulated objects from single RGB videos, overcoming limitations of existing methods that typically require multiple views or pre-scanned objects. It leverages foundation model priors and introduces Adaptive Sampling Refinement (ASR) to accurately optimize the scale and pose of objects in a 3D space. Additionally, it employs a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) to enhance hand-object alignment by using reasoning about contact points as optimization constraints. The framework is validated through extensive experiments and the introduction of two new datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in diverse scenarios.

zh.title: 从单目视频重建4D人-物体交互的新方法
zh.desc: ArtHOI是一个基于优化的框架，旨在从单一的单目RGB视频中重建4D人类与物体的交互。该方法结合了基础模型的先验知识，通过自适应采样优化和多模态语言模型指导来提高重建精度。与传统的手-物体交互方法不同，ArtHOI能够处理动态物体，而无需预先扫描或多视角视频。我们还提供了两个新数据集，以便全面评估该方法的有效性和鲁棒性。

papers.title: SeGPruner: Semantic-Geometric Visual Token Pruner for 3D Question Answering
papers.abstract: Abstract A semantic-aware and geometry-guided token pruning framework is presented for efficient 3D question answering with multi-view images, achieving significant reductions in token budget and inference latency while maintaining competitive performance.  AI-generated summary Vision-language models (VLMs) have been widely adopted for 3D question answering (3D QA). In typical pipelines, visual tokens extracted from multiple viewpoints are concatenated with language tokens and jointly processed by a large language model (LLM) for inference. However, aggregating multi-view observations inevitably introduces severe token redundancy, leading to an overly large visual token set that significantly hinders inference efficiency under constrained token budgets. Visual token pruning has emerged as a prevalent strategy to address this issue. Nevertheless, most existing pruners are primarily tailored to 2D inputs or rely on indirect geometric cues, which limits their ability to explicitly retain semantically critical objects and maintain sufficient spatial coverage for robust 3D reasoning. In this paper, we propose SeGPruner, a semantic-aware and geometry-guided token reduction framework for efficient 3D QA with multi-view images. Specifically, SeGPruner first preserves semantically salient tokens through an attention-based importance module (Saliency-aware Token Selector), ensuring that object-critical evidence is retained. It then complements these tokens with spatially diverse ones via a geometry-guided selector (Geometry-aware Token Diversifier), which jointly considers semantic relevance and 3D geometric distance. This cooperation between saliency preservation and geometry-guided diversification balances object-level evidence and global scene coverage under aggressive token reduction. Extensive experiments on ScanQA and OpenEQA demonstrate that SeGPruner substantially improves inference efficiency, reducing the visual token budget by 91% and inference latency by 86%, while maintaining competitive performance in 3D reasoning tasks.

ru.title: Семантическое и геометрическое отсечение токенов для эффективного 3D-рассуждения
ru.desc: В работе предлагается SeGPruner — фреймворк для эффективного сокращения визуальных токенов при ответах на вопросы о 3D-сценах с использованием мультивидовых изображений. Метод комбинирует два подхода: селектор токенов на основе внимания сохраняет семантически важные элементы сцены, а геометрический диверсификатор добавляет пространственно разнообразные токены, учитывая 3D-расстояния. Такое сочетание семантической осведомленности и геометрического руководства позволяет поддерживать баланс между детализацией объектов и полнотой покрытия сцены при агрессивном сжатии. Экспериментальные результаты показывают сокращение визуального бюджета токенов на 91% и задержку вывода на 86% при сохранении качества решения задач трехмерного рассуждения.

en.title: Efficient 3D QA with Semantic and Geometry-Aware Token Pruning
en.desc: This paper introduces SeGPruner, a framework designed to enhance the efficiency of 3D question answering (QA) using multi-view images. It addresses the problem of token redundancy that arises when visual tokens from different viewpoints are combined, which can slow down inference in large language models. SeGPruner employs a two-step approach: it first selects important tokens based on their semantic relevance and then adds spatially diverse tokens to ensure comprehensive scene coverage. The results show that this method significantly reduces the number of visual tokens and inference time while still performing well in 3D reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 高效3D问答的语义与几何剪枝
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种语义感知和几何引导的令牌剪枝框架SeGPruner，用于高效的3D问答。该框架通过关注重要性模块保留语义显著的令牌，并通过几何引导选择器补充空间多样的令牌，从而在激进的令牌减少下平衡对象级证据和全局场景覆盖。实验结果表明，SeGPruner在ScanQA和OpenEQA数据集上显著提高了推理效率，视觉令牌预算减少了91%，推理延迟减少了86%。同时，它在3D推理任务中保持了竞争力的性能。

papers.title: Colon-Bench: An Agentic Workflow for Scalable Dense Lesion Annotation in Full-Procedure Colonoscopy Videos
papers.abstract: Abstract A new benchmark dataset called Colon-Bench is introduced for evaluating multimodal large language models in colon cancer screening, featuring comprehensive annotations for multiple lesion types in endoscopic videos.  AI-generated summary Early screening via colonoscopy is critical for colon cancer prevention, yet developing robust AI systems for this domain is hindered by the lack of densely annotated, long-sequence video datasets. Existing datasets predominantly focus on single-class polyp detection and lack the rich spatial, temporal, and linguistic annotations required to evaluate modern Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). To address this critical gap, we introduce Colon-Bench, generated via a novel multi-stage agentic workflow. Our pipeline seamlessly integrates temporal proposals, bounding-box tracking, AI-driven visual confirmation, and human-in-the-loop review to scalably annotate full-procedure videos. The resulting verified benchmark is unprecedented in scope, encompassing 528 videos, 14 distinct lesion categories (including polyps, ulcers, and bleeding), over 300,000 bounding boxes, 213,000 segmentation masks, and 133,000 words of clinical descriptions. We utilize Colon-Bench to rigorously evaluate state-of-the-art MLLMs across lesion classification, Open-Vocabulary Video Object Segmentation (OV-VOS), and video Visual Question Answering (VQA). The MLLM results demonstrate surprisingly high localization performance in medical domains compared to SAM-3. Finally, we analyze common VQA errors from MLLMs to introduce a novel "colon-skill" prompting strategy, improving zero-shot MLLM performance by up to 9.7% across most MLLMs. The dataset and the code are available at https://abdullahamdi.com/colon-bench .
affiliations: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)

ru.title: Мультимодальные модели для точной диагностики поражений толстой кишки
ru.desc: Представлена новая эталонная база данных Colon-Bench для оценки мультимодальных больших языковых моделей в области скрининга рака толстой кишки. Датасет содержит полные видео колоноскопии с плотными аннотациями более чем 14 типов поражений, включая полипы, язвы и кровотечения, а также более 300 тысяч ограничивающих рамок и 133 тысячи клинических описаний. Для создания датасета использован многоэтапный рабочий процесс с агентными системами, которые автоматически предлагают локализации поражений, отслеживают их во времени и проверяют визуальные предсказания с участием человека. Проведённая оценка современных MLLM показала, что эти модели демонстрируют высокую производительность в медицинских задачах локализации, а предложенная стратегия prompt engineering улучшает результаты на 9.7 процента.

en.title: Colon-Bench: Advancing AI in Colon Cancer Screening
en.desc: The paper presents Colon-Bench, a new benchmark dataset designed for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in the context of colon cancer screening. This dataset addresses the limitations of existing datasets by providing extensive annotations for various lesion types in endoscopic videos, including polyp detection and other abnormalities. The authors developed a comprehensive annotation pipeline that combines automated processes with human oversight, resulting in a rich dataset of 528 videos and detailed annotations. The study demonstrates the effectiveness of MLLMs in tasks like lesion classification and video visual question answering, while also introducing a novel prompting strategy to enhance model performance.

zh.title: Colon-Bench：结肠癌筛查的多模态评估新基准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个新的基准数据集Colon-Bench，用于评估多模态大型语言模型在结肠癌筛查中的表现。该数据集包含528个视频，涵盖14种不同的病变类型，并提供了丰富的空间、时间和语言注释。通过创新的多阶段工作流程，Colon-Bench实现了对全程视频的高效标注，解决了现有数据集中缺乏密集注释的问题。研究表明，使用Colon-Bench可以有效评估最先进的多模态大型语言模型在病变分类和视频问答等任务中的性能。

papers.title: Ghost-FWL: A Large-Scale Full-Waveform LiDAR Dataset for Ghost Detection and Removal
papers.abstract: Abstract Full-waveform LiDAR enables improved ghost point removal through a large-scale dataset and self-supervised learning approach, enhancing localization and object detection accuracy.  AI-generated summary LiDAR has become an essential sensing modality in autonomous driving, robotics, and smart-city applications. However, ghost points (or ghosts), which are false reflections caused by multi-path laser returns from glass and reflective surfaces, severely degrade 3D mapping and localization accuracy. Prior ghost removal relies on geometric consistency in dense point clouds, failing on mobile LiDAR's sparse, dynamic data. We address this by exploiting full-waveform LiDAR (FWL), which captures complete temporal intensity profiles rather than just peak distances, providing crucial cues for distinguishing ghosts from genuine reflections in mobile scenarios. As this is a new task, we present Ghost-FWL, the first and largest annotated mobile FWL dataset for ghost detection and removal. Ghost-FWL comprises 24K frames across 10 diverse scenes with 7.5 billion peak-level annotations, which is 100x larger than existing annotated FWL datasets. Benefiting from this large-scale dataset, we establish a FWL-based baseline model for ghost detection and propose FWL-MAE, a masked autoencoder for efficient self-supervised representation learning on FWL data. Experiments show that our baseline outperforms existing methods in ghost removal accuracy, and our ghost removal further enhances downstream tasks such as LiDAR-based SLAM (66% trajectory error reduction) and 3D object detection (50x false positive reduction). The dataset and code is publicly available and can be accessed via the project page: https://keio-csg.github.io/Ghost-FWL

ru.title: Волны истины: удаление призрачных отражений в полноволновом LiDAR
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод удаления призрачных точек в LiDAR данных, полученных мобильными сенсорами, путем использования полноволновой LiDAR информации, которая захватывает полные временные профили интенсивности отражений. Авторы представили Ghost-FWL — первый и крупнейший аннотированный датасет полноволновых LiDAR данных, содержащий 24 тысячи кадров и 7.5 миллиардов аннотаций точек. Для обработки этих данных разработан базовой алгоритм обнаружения призрачных точек и предложена архитектура FWL-MAE, использующая маскированный автокодировщик для самообучения представлений полноволновых данных. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение в удалении артефактов (на 66% снижена ошибка траектории SLAM и на 50x уменьшено количество ложных срабатываний в 3D детекции объектов).

en.title: Enhancing LiDAR Accuracy by Eliminating Ghost Points with Self-Supervised Learning
en.desc: This paper presents a novel approach to removing ghost points in full-waveform LiDAR data using a large-scale self-supervised learning framework. Ghost points are misleading reflections that can significantly impair the accuracy of 3D mapping and localization in applications like autonomous driving. The authors introduce Ghost-FWL, a comprehensive dataset with 24,000 frames and billions of annotations, which is crucial for training models to distinguish between genuine reflections and ghost points. Their proposed FWL-MAE model demonstrates superior performance in ghost removal, leading to significant improvements in downstream tasks such as SLAM and 3D object detection.

zh.title: 全波形LiDAR：提升虚假点去除与定位精度的创新方法
zh.desc: 全波形LiDAR技术通过大规模数据集和自监督学习方法，显著提高了虚假点的去除效果，从而增强了定位和物体检测的准确性。虚假点是由激光在玻璃和反射表面上的多路径反射造成的错误反射，严重影响了三维地图和定位的精度。我们提出了Ghost-FWL，这是第一个也是最大的移动全波形LiDAR数据集，专注于虚假点的检测和去除，包含24000帧和75亿个注释。通过利用这一数据集，我们建立了基于全波形的基线模型，并提出了FWL-MAE，一个高效的自监督表示学习方法，实验结果表明我们的模型在虚假点去除准确性上优于现有方法。

papers.title: MPDiT: Multi-Patch Global-to-Local Transformer Architecture For Efficient Flow Matching and Diffusion Model
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-patch transformer design for diffusion models reduces computational cost by processing different patch sizes across network layers while maintaining generative performance.  AI-generated summary Transformer architectures, particularly Diffusion Transformers (DiTs), have become widely used in diffusion and flow-matching models due to their strong performance compared to convolutional UNets. However, the isotropic design of DiTs processes the same number of patchified tokens in every block, leading to relatively heavy computation during training process. In this work, we introduce a multi-patch transformer design in which early blocks operate on larger patches to capture coarse global context, while later blocks use smaller patches to refine local details. This hierarchical design could reduces computational cost by up to 50\% in GFLOPs while achieving good generative performance. In addition, we also propose improved designs for time and class embeddings that accelerate training convergence. Extensive experiments on the ImageNet dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of our architectural choices. Code is released at https://github.com/quandao10/MPDiT

ru.title: Иерархическая многопатчевая архитектура для эффективных диффузионных трансформеров
ru.desc: В статье предлагается многопатчевая архитектура трансформера для диффузионных моделей, которая использует патчи разного размера на разных слоях сети. Ранние блоки обрабатывают крупные патчи для захвата глобального контекста, а поздние блоки работают с мелкими патчами для уточнения локальных деталей. Такой иерархический подход снижает вычислительные затраты на 50% по метрике GFLOPs без потери качества генерации. Авторы также предлагают улучшенные схемы эмбеддинга для временных шагов и классов, которые ускоряют сходимость обучения.

en.title: Efficient Multi-Patch Transformers for Enhanced Diffusion Models
en.desc: This paper presents a multi-patch transformer design for diffusion models that optimizes computational efficiency while preserving generative quality. By using larger patches in the early layers to capture broad context and smaller patches in later layers for detailed refinement, the model reduces computational costs significantly. The proposed architecture can decrease the required GFLOPs by up to 50% compared to traditional designs. Additionally, enhancements to time and class embeddings are introduced to speed up training convergence, with experiments validating the approach on the ImageNet dataset.

zh.title: 多补丁变换器：高效生成的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种多补丁变换器设计，用于扩散模型，通过在网络层中处理不同大小的补丁来降低计算成本，同时保持生成性能。与传统的卷积UNet相比，扩散变换器（DiTs）在扩散和流匹配模型中表现出色，但其各层处理相同数量的补丁令计算负担较重。我们设计的多补丁变换器在早期层使用较大补丁捕捉全局上下文，而后期层则使用较小补丁细化局部细节。实验结果表明，该设计在保持良好生成性能的同时，计算成本可降低多达50%。

papers.title: Distilling Conversations: Abstract Compression of Conversational Audio Context for LLM-based ASR
papers.abstract: Abstract Multimodal conversational context improves LLM-based speech recognition, and abstract compression reduces computational overhead by replacing audio sequences with learned latent tokens while preserving transcripts.  AI-generated summary Standard LLM-based speech recognition systems typically process utterances in isolation, limiting their ability to leverage conversational context. In this work, we study whether multimodal context from prior turns improves LLM-based ASR and how to represent that context efficiently. We find that, after supervised multi-turn training, conversational context mainly helps with the recognition of contextual entities. However, conditioning on raw context is expensive because the prior-turn audio token sequence grows rapidly with conversation length. To address this, we propose Abstract Compression, which replaces the audio portion of prior turns with a fixed number of learned latent tokens while retaining corresponding transcripts explicitly. On both in-domain and out-of-domain test sets, the compressed model recovers part of the gains of raw-context conditioning with a smaller prior-turn audio footprint. We also provide targeted analyses of the compression setup and its trade-offs.
affiliations: Brno University of Technology, Czech Republic

ru.title: Сжатие аудиоконтекста для эффективного распознавания речи в диалогах
ru.desc: Статья исследует, как контекст из предыдущих реплик диалога улучшает распознавание речи на основе больших языковых моделей. Авторы обнаруживают, что многомодальный контекст особенно помогает распознавать контекстные сущности. Они предлагают метод Abstract Compression, который заменяет аудиопоследовательности предыдущих реплик компактными векторами скрытого пространства, оставляя при этом явно текстовые транскрипции. Такой подход позволяет значительно снизить вычислительные затраты, сохраняя часть преимуществ от использования полного контекста.

en.title: Enhancing Speech Recognition with Efficient Context Compression
en.desc: This paper explores how using conversational context can enhance speech recognition systems based on large language models (LLMs). It identifies that traditional systems often treat each speech input separately, missing out on valuable information from previous interactions. The authors introduce a method called Abstract Compression, which simplifies the representation of prior audio by using learned latent tokens, thus reducing the computational load. Their findings show that this approach maintains recognition accuracy while minimizing the amount of audio data processed, making it more efficient for longer conversations.

zh.title: 多模态上下文提升语音识别效率
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了多模态对话上下文如何改善基于大语言模型（LLM）的语音识别。我们提出了一种抽象压缩方法，通过用学习到的潜在标记替换音频序列，来减少计算开销，同时保留转录文本。经过多轮监督训练后，我们发现对话上下文主要有助于识别上下文实体。我们的压缩模型在保持较小的音频占用的同时，能够恢复部分原始上下文条件的优势。

papers.title: It Takes Two: A Duet of Periodicity and Directionality for Burst Flicker Removal
papers.abstract: Abstract Flicker artifacts in short-exposure photography are addressed through a transformer-based architecture that exploits periodicity and directionality characteristics for effective flicker removal without ghosting.  AI-generated summary Flicker artifacts, arising from unstable illumination and row-wise exposure inconsistencies, pose a significant challenge in short-exposure photography, severely degrading image quality. Unlike typical artifacts, e.g., noise and low-light, flicker is a structured degradation with specific spatial-temporal patterns, which are not accounted for in current generic restoration frameworks, leading to suboptimal flicker suppression and ghosting artifacts. In this work, we reveal that flicker artifacts exhibit two intrinsic characteristics, periodicity and directionality, and propose Flickerformer, a transformer-based architecture that effectively removes flicker without introducing ghosting. Specifically, Flickerformer comprises three key components: a phase-based fusion module (PFM), an autocorrelation feed-forward network (AFFN), and a wavelet-based directional attention module (WDAM). Based on the periodicity, PFM performs inter-frame phase correlation to adaptively aggregate burst features, while AFFN exploits intra-frame structural regularities through autocorrelation, jointly enhancing the network's ability to perceive spatially recurring patterns. Moreover, motivated by the directionality of flicker artifacts, WDAM leverages high-frequency variations in the wavelet domain to guide the restoration of low-frequency dark regions, yielding precise localization of flicker artifacts. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Flickerformer outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in both quantitative metrics and visual quality. The source code is available at https://github.com/qulishen/Flickerformer.
affiliations: College of Computer Science, Nankai University
Nankai International Advanced Research Institute (SHENZHEN FUTIAN)

ru.title: Трансформер для устранения мерцания через анализ периодичности и направленности
ru.desc: В работе предлагается Flickerformer — архитектура на основе трансформера для удаления артефактов мерцания в короткой экспозиции. Авторы выявили две ключевые характеристики мерцания: периодичность и направленность, и разработали специализированную нейронную сеть, включающую модуль фазовой фузии для адаптивной агрегации кадров и сеть автокорреляции для выделения внутрикадровых закономерностей. Волновой модуль с направленным вниманием использует высокочастотные вариации для точной локализации артефактов мерцания в низкочастотных областях. Экспериментальные результаты подтверждают превосходство предложенного подхода над существующими методами восстановления изображений без появления артефактов призрачности.

en.title: Flickerformer: Transforming Flicker Removal in Photography
en.desc: This paper presents Flickerformer, a novel transformer-based architecture designed to effectively remove flicker artifacts in short-exposure photography. Flicker artifacts are caused by unstable lighting and exposure inconsistencies, which create structured degradation patterns that traditional restoration methods struggle to address. The proposed model utilizes periodicity and directionality characteristics of flicker to enhance restoration without introducing ghosting effects. Key components of Flickerformer include a phase-based fusion module, an autocorrelation feed-forward network, and a wavelet-based directional attention module, all of which work together to improve image quality significantly compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 闪烁去除，清晰无影
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种基于变换器的架构Flickerformer，用于去除短曝光摄影中的闪烁伪影。闪烁伪影是由于光照不稳定和行间曝光不一致造成的，具有特定的时空模式。Flickerformer利用闪烁伪影的周期性和方向性特征，通过相位融合模块、自相关前馈网络和小波方向注意模块，有效去除闪烁而不产生重影。实验结果表明，Flickerformer在定量指标和视觉质量上均优于现有的最先进方法。

papers.title: When Documents Disagree: Measuring Institutional Variation in Transplant Guidance with Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract  Patient education materials for solid-organ transplantation vary substantially across U.S. centers, yet no systematic method exists to quantify this heterogeneity at scale. We introduce a framework that grounds the same patient questions in different centers' handbooks using retrieval-augmented language models and compares the resulting answers using a five-label consistency taxonomy. Applied to 102 handbooks from 23 centers and 1,115 benchmark questions, the framework quantifies heterogeneity across four dimensions: question, topic, organ, and center. We find that 20.8% of non-absent pairwise comparisons exhibit clinically meaningful divergence, concentrated in condition monitoring and lifestyle topics. Coverage gaps are even more prominent: 96.2% of question-handbook pairs miss relevant content, with reproductive health at 95.1% absence. Center-level divergence profiles are stable and interpretable, where heterogeneity reflects systematic institutional differences, likely due to patient diversity. These findings expose an information gap in transplant patient education materials, with document-grounded medical question answering highlighting opportunities for content improvement.
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University, Pittsburgh, PA, USA

ru.title: Выявление информационных пробелов в образовательных материалах для пациентов с трансплантацией органов
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали фреймворк на основе retrieval-augmented language models для анализа несогласованности в материалах для обучения пациентов трансплантации органов в разных медицинских центрах США. Система использует семантический поиск для сопоставления одних и тех же вопросов пациентов с информацией из справочников разных центров и сравнивает ответы с помощью таксономии из пяти категорий согласованности. Анализ 102 справочников из 23 центров выявил значительные пробелы в охвате информации — 96.2% вопросов пациентов не получают полного ответа в имеющихся материалах, особенно в темах репродуктивного здоровья. Результаты показывают, что различия между центрами отражают систематические различия в подходах учреждений и подчеркивают необходимость улучшения содержания образовательных материалов.

en.title: Bridging the Gap in Transplant Patient Education
en.desc: This paper presents a framework that uses retrieval-augmented language models to analyze and compare patient education materials for solid-organ transplantation across different U.S. centers. By applying this framework to 102 handbooks and 1,115 benchmark questions, the authors quantify the variability in the information provided, identifying significant inconsistencies in answers related to condition monitoring and lifestyle topics. The study reveals that a large percentage of question-handbook pairs lack relevant content, particularly in areas like reproductive health. Overall, the findings highlight the need for improved consistency and coverage in transplant patient education materials, driven by the diverse needs of patients across different centers.

zh.title: 量化器官移植教育材料的异质性
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种框架，用于量化美国不同中心的器官移植患者教育材料的异质性。通过使用增强检索的语言模型，我们将相同的患者问题与不同中心的手册进行对比，并使用五级一致性分类法来评估答案的差异。研究发现，20.8%的比较结果在临床上具有重要意义，尤其集中在病情监测和生活方式主题上。此外，96.2%的问题与手册对缺乏相关内容，生殖健康问题的缺失率高达95.1%。

papers.title: TrajectoryMover: Generative Movement of Object Trajectories in Videos
papers.abstract: Abstract TrajectoryAtlas enables generative video editing by generating large-scale synthetic paired video data and training a video generator to move object 3D motion trajectories while preserving plausibility and identity.  AI-generated summary Generative video editing has enabled several intuitive editing operations for short video clips that would previously have been difficult to achieve, especially for non-expert editors. Existing methods focus on prescribing an object's 3D or 2D motion trajectory in a video, or on altering the appearance of an object or a scene, while preserving both the video's plausibility and identity. Yet a method to move an object's 3D motion trajectory in a video, i.e., moving an object while preserving its relative 3D motion, is currently still missing. The main challenge lies in obtaining paired video data for this scenario. Previous methods typically rely on clever data generation approaches to construct plausible paired data from unpaired videos, but this approach fails if one of the videos in a pair can not easily be constructed from the other. Instead, we introduce TrajectoryAtlas, a new data generation pipeline for large-scale synthetic paired video data and a video generator TrajectoryMover fine-tuned with this data. We show that this successfully enables generative movement of object trajectories. Project page: https://chhatrekiran.github.io/trajectorymover

ru.title: Управление траекториями объектов через синтетическую генерацию данных
ru.desc: TrajectoryAtlas представляет новый подход к редактированию видео, позволяющий перемещать 3D траектории движения объектов в видеозаписях. Авторы создали конвейер синтетической генерации данных для производства больших объёмов парных видеоданных, необходимых для обучения модели. На основе этих синтетических данных они обучили генеративную модель TrajectoryMover, которая может изменять траектории объектов, сохраняя при этом правдоподобность и идентичность объекта. Метод решает критическую проблему отсутствия парных видеоданных, которые было невозможно получить из непарных видео традиционными способами.

en.title: Revolutionizing Video Editing with 3D Motion Control
en.desc: TrajectoryAtlas is a novel approach in generative video editing that focuses on creating large-scale synthetic paired video data. It trains a video generator called TrajectoryMover to manipulate 3D motion trajectories of objects while maintaining their identity and the overall plausibility of the video. This method addresses the challenge of moving an object's 3D trajectory, which has been a gap in existing techniques that either alter appearance or prescribe motion. By generating paired data effectively, TrajectoryAtlas allows for intuitive editing operations that were previously difficult for non-expert users.

zh.title: 生成视频编辑的新方法：TrajectoryAtlas
zh.desc: TrajectoryAtlas 是一种生成视频编辑的方法，它通过生成大规模的合成配对视频数据来训练视频生成器，从而在保持视频的合理性和物体身份的前提下，移动物体的三维运动轨迹。现有的方法主要集中在规定物体的三维或二维运动轨迹，或改变物体或场景的外观，但缺乏在视频中移动物体三维运动轨迹的有效方法。我们提出的 TrajectoryAtlas 解决了这一问题，提供了一种新的数据生成管道，能够生成合成的配对视频数据，并通过微调的 TrajectoryMover 生成物体轨迹的运动。我们的研究表明，这种方法成功实现了物体轨迹的生成性运动。

papers.title: TokenDial: Continuous Attribute Control in Text-to-Video via Spatiotemporal Token Offsets
papers.abstract: Abstract TokenDial enables precise attribute control in text-to-video models by using additive offsets in spatiotemporal token space for coherent edits without retraining.  AI-generated summary We present TokenDial, a framework for continuous, slider-style attribute control in pretrained text-to-video generation models. While modern generators produce strong holistic videos, they offer limited control over how much an attribute changes (e.g., effect intensity or motion magnitude) without drifting identity, background, or temporal coherence. TokenDial is built on the observation: additive offsets in the intermediate spatiotemporal visual patch-token space form a semantic control direction, where adjusting the offset magnitude yields coherent, predictable edits for both appearance and motion dynamics. We learn attribute-specific token offsets without retraining the backbone, using pretrained understanding signals: semantic direction matching for appearance and motion-magnitude scaling for motion. We demonstrate TokenDial's effectiveness on diverse attributes and prompts, achieving stronger controllability and higher-quality edits than state-of-the-art baselines, supported by extensive quantitative evaluation and human studies.

ru.title: Слайдеры для видео: управляемое редактирование без переобучения
ru.desc: TokenDial — это фреймворк для точного управления атрибутами в предобученных моделях генерации видео из текста. Метод использует аддитивные смещения в пространстве промежуточных пространственно-временных токенов патчей, что позволяет найти семантические направления управления. Авторы обучают смещения токенов для конкретных атрибутов без переобучения основной модели, используя семантическое соответствие направлений и масштабирование величины движения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что TokenDial обеспечивает более точное управление и лучшее качество редактирования по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: TokenDial: Mastering Attribute Control in Text-to-Video Generation
en.desc: TokenDial is a novel framework that enhances control over attributes in text-to-video generation models without the need for retraining. It utilizes additive offsets in the spatiotemporal token space, allowing users to make precise adjustments to video attributes like intensity and motion while maintaining coherence. By learning specific token offsets for different attributes, TokenDial ensures that changes do not disrupt the video's identity or background. The framework has been shown to outperform existing methods in terms of controllability and edit quality, as validated by both quantitative metrics and human evaluations.

zh.title: TokenDial：精确控制文本到视频生成的属性
zh.desc: TokenDial 是一个框架，允许在预训练的文本到视频生成模型中进行连续的属性控制。它通过在时空标记空间中使用加性偏移，能够实现精确的编辑，而无需重新训练模型。该方法利用中间视觉补丁标记空间中的偏移量，形成语义控制方向，从而实现对外观和运动动态的可预测编辑。实验表明，TokenDial 在多种属性和提示下表现出更强的可控性和更高质量的编辑效果。

papers.title: Tabular LLMs for Interpretable Few-Shot Alzheimer's Disease Prediction with Multimodal Biomedical Data
papers.abstract: Abstract A domain-adapted tabular large language model framework demonstrates improved few-shot Alzheimer's disease classification performance over traditional methods while maintaining stability under missing data conditions.  AI-generated summary Accurate diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease (AD) requires handling tabular biomarker data, yet such data are often small and incomplete, where deep learning models frequently fail to outperform classical methods. Pretrained large language models (LLMs) offer few-shot generalization, structured reasoning, and interpretable outputs, providing a powerful paradigm shift for clinical prediction. We propose TAP-GPT Tabular Alzheimer's Prediction GPT, a domain-adapted tabular LLM framework built on TableGPT2 and fine-tuned for few-shot AD classification using tabular prompts rather than plain texts. We evaluate TAP-GPT across four ADNI-derived datasets, including QT-PAD biomarkers and region-level structural MRI, amyloid PET, and tau PET for binary AD classification. Across multimodal and unimodal settings, TAP-GPT improves upon its backbone models and outperforms traditional machine learning baselines in the few-shot setting while remaining competitive with state-of-the-art general-purpose LLMs. We show that feature selection mitigates degradation in high-dimensional inputs and that TAP-GPT maintains stable performance under simulated and real-world missingness without imputation. Additionally, TAP-GPT produces structured, modality-aware reasoning aligned with established AD biology and shows greater stability under self-reflection, supporting its use in iterative multi-agent systems. To our knowledge, this is the first systematic application of a tabular-specialized LLM to multimodal biomarker-based AD prediction, demonstrating that such pretrained models can effectively address structured clinical prediction tasks and laying the foundation for tabular LLM-driven multi-agent clinical decision-support systems. The source code is publicly available on GitHub: https://github.com/sophie-kearney/TAP-GPT.
affiliations: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

ru.title: LLM для табулярных данных: диагностика Альцгеймера без полноты информации
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали TAP-GPT - специализированную языковую модель для анализа табулярных медицинских данных, адаптированную для диагностики болезни Альцгеймера. Модель обучена на небольшом количестве примеров и показывает лучшие результаты, чем традиционные методы машинного обучения при работе с биомаркерами и данными МРТ. Ключевое преимущество TAP-GPT заключается в её устойчивости к пропускам в данных без необходимости заполнения отсутствующих значений. Модель генерирует структурированные объяснения своих предсказаний, что делает её пригодной для использования в системах поддержки клинических решений.

en.title: Revolutionizing Alzheimer's Diagnosis with TAP-GPT!
en.desc: This paper presents TAP-GPT, a specialized large language model designed for classifying Alzheimer's disease using tabular data. It leverages few-shot learning capabilities to improve performance over traditional machine learning methods, especially in scenarios with incomplete data. The model is fine-tuned to handle multimodal inputs, such as biomarkers and imaging data, while maintaining stability and interpretability. TAP-GPT demonstrates significant advancements in clinical prediction tasks, paving the way for future applications in multi-agent decision-support systems.

zh.title: 表格大语言模型助力阿尔茨海默病分类
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为TAP-GPT的领域适应性表格大语言模型框架，旨在提高阿尔茨海默病的少样本分类性能。该模型利用预训练的大语言模型，能够处理小规模和不完整的生物标志物数据，克服传统深度学习模型的局限性。通过在多个数据集上进行评估，TAP-GPT在少样本设置下超越了传统机器学习基线，并在多模态和单模态环境中保持竞争力。该研究为基于表格的大语言模型在临床决策支持系统中的应用奠定了基础。
